evals.prompt: Generate a trend-analysis report from these findings about open-source AI adoption in enterprise settings. The findings are in /reports/ai-enterprise/findings/ and the topic covers the last 18 months of data through 2026-Q1. I need the full deliverable with trajectory, signals, scenarios, and a diagram.
evals.expected_output: A complete trend-analysis document with all five required sections (Trajectory, Signals, Drivers & Inhibitors, Scenarios, Implications & Watch-list), front matter with title/date/horizon, a Mermaid or time-series diagram, and inline citations. No MIF URNs leaked into the narrative.

files.content: {"@context":"https://mif-spec.dev/schema/context.jsonld","@type":"Concept","@id":"urn:mif:concept:ai-enterprise:f_trajectory_1","namespace":"ai-enterprise","title":"Enterprise open-source AI adoption accelerating since 2024","content":"Enterprise adoption of open-source LLMs rose from 18% to 41% between Q1 2024 and Q1 2026, driven by cost reduction and regulatory pressure to avoid vendor lock-in.","summary":"Enterprise OS-AI adoption doubled in two years.","created":"2026-03-15T00:00:00Z","tags":["adoption","enterprise","open-source"],"provenance":{"@type":"Provenance","sourceType":"empirical","confidence":0.88,"trustLevel":"high_confidence"},"citations":[{"@type":"Citation","citationType":"report","citationRole":"primary","title":"State of Enterprise AI 2026","url":"https://example.com/state-of-enterprise-ai-2026","accessed":"2026-03-20"}],"extensions":{"harness":{"dimension":"trajectory","verification":{"verdict":"survived","verdict_basis":"Multiple independent surveys corroborate the adoption figures."}}}}

{"@context":"https://mif-spec.dev/schema/context.jsonld","@type":"Concept","@id":"urn:mif:concept:ai-enterprise:f_signals_1","namespace":"ai-enterprise","title":"GPU procurement by enterprises tripled as a leading signal","content":"Data center GPU orders from Fortune 500 companies tripled YoY in 2025, a leading indicator of increased on-premise AI inference capacity.","summary":"GPU orders signal accelerating on-prem AI investment.","created":"2026-02-01T00:00:00Z","tags":["signals","gpu","infrastructure"],"provenance":{"@type":"Provenance","sourceType":"empirical","confidence":0.82,"trustLevel":"high_confidence"},"citations":[{"@type":"Citation","citationType":"news","citationRole":"primary","title":"GPU Demand Surge 2025","url":"https://example.com/gpu-demand-2025","accessed":"2026-02-10"}],"extensions":{"harness":{"dimension":"signals","verification":{"verdict":"survived","verdict_basis":"Semiconductor shipment data corroborates the GPU demand figure."}}}}

{"@context":"https://mif-spec.dev/schema/context.jsonld","@type":"Concept","@id":"urn:mif:concept:ai-enterprise:f_signals_2","namespace":"ai-enterprise","title":"Open-source model capability parity reached in coding tasks","content":"By mid-2025 open-source models matched proprietary frontier model performance on standard coding benchmarks (HumanEval: 87% vs 89%). This parity is a lagging confirmation of the adoption trend.","summary":"OS model parity on coding benchmarks confirmed by mid-2025.","created":"2026-01-10T00:00:00Z","tags":["signals","benchmarks","capability"],"provenance":{"@type":"Provenance","sourceType":"empirical","confidence":0.79,"trustLevel":"high_confidence"},"citations":[{"@type":"Citation","citationType":"benchmark","citationRole":"primary","title":"HumanEval Leaderboard 2025","url":"https://example.com/humaneval-2025","accessed":"2026-01-15"}],"extensions":{"harness":{"dimension":"signals","verification":{"verdict":"weakened","verdict_basis":"Benchmark methodology disputed; cherry-picking concerns raised."}}}}

{"@context":"https://mif-spec.dev/schema/context.jsonld","@type":"Concept","@id":"urn:mif:concept:ai-enterprise:f_falsified_1","namespace":"ai-enterprise","title":"Enterprises uniformly prefer open-source over proprietary","content":"A survey claimed 95% of enterprises prefer open-source AI unconditionally.","summary":"Claimed 95% preference was not supported.","created":"2026-01-05T00:00:00Z","tags":["adoption"],"provenance":{"@type":"Provenance","sourceType":"survey","confidence":0.2,"trustLevel":"low_confidence"},"citations":[{"@type":"Citation","citationType":"survey","citationRole":"primary","title":"Biased Survey Report","url":"https://example.com/biased-survey","accessed":"2026-01-06"}],"extensions":{"harness":{"dimension":"trajectory","verification":{"verdict":"falsified","verdict_basis":"Survey methodology was vendor-sponsored and contradicted by independent data."}}}}

deterministic_checks.description: Output contains required Trajectory section heading
Output contains required Signals section heading
Output contains required Scenarios section heading
Output contains required Drivers & Inhibitors section heading
Output contains required Implications & Watch-list section heading
MIF URNs must not leak into narrative output
Falsified finding must be excluded from report
Output contains a diagram block or at least inline citations

expectations.description: The Trajectory section states a direction of change (increasing/decreasing/steady) and is anchored to a specific date range, not undated assertions
expectations.criteria: Trajectory section must state directional movement (increasing, accelerating, rising, or equivalent) with an explicit date or time range (e.g. 2024–2026, Q1 2024 to Q1 2026)

expectations.description: Signals section distinguishes leading from lagging signals for at least one signal
expectations.criteria: At least one signal is classified as 'leading' and at least one as 'lagging' (or equivalent language)

expectations.description: Weakened finding (f_signals_2) is included but flagged with a confidence caveat, not silently presented as fact
expectations.criteria: The benchmark parity finding is present but includes a qualification (e.g. 'disputed', 'contested', 'weakened', 'caveated', 'with uncertainty') rather than being stated as established fact

expectations.description: Scenarios section presents 2–4 named forward paths with conditions or triggers, not a single deterministic forecast
expectations.criteria: At least two distinct scenario paths are named with conditions or trigger signals, and a time horizon is stated

evals.prompt: I only have two findings for the quantum-computing adoption topic — they're at /reports/quantum/findings/. Can you still produce a trend-analysis deliverable? I know it'll be thin. Just do what you can with the horizon set to 5 years.
evals.expected_output: A minimal but structurally complete trend-analysis document covering all five sections, a 5-year horizon stated in front matter, and a diagram (even a simple one). The skill should surface the thinness of the evidence rather than hallucinate signals not in the findings.

files.content: {"@context":"https://mif-spec.dev/schema/context.jsonld","@type":"Concept","@id":"urn:mif:concept:quantum:f_trajectory_1","namespace":"quantum","title":"Quantum hardware error rates falling but above fault-tolerance threshold","content":"Physical qubit error rates fell from ~1% in 2022 to ~0.1% in 2026, still above the ~0.01% threshold needed for fault-tolerant computation.","summary":"Error rates improving but fault tolerance not yet reached.","created":"2026-04-01T00:00:00Z","tags":["trajectory","hardware"],"provenance":{"@type":"Provenance","sourceType":"empirical","confidence":0.9,"trustLevel":"high_confidence"},"citations":[{"@type":"Citation","citationType":"paper","citationRole":"primary","title":"Qubit Error Rate Survey 2026","url":"https://example.com/qubit-error-2026","accessed":"2026-04-05"}],"extensions":{"harness":{"dimension":"trajectory","verification":{"verdict":"survived","verdict_basis":"Reproduced by multiple hardware vendor reports."}}}}

{"@context":"https://mif-spec.dev/schema/context.jsonld","@type":"Concept","@id":"urn:mif:concept:quantum:f_signals_1","namespace":"quantum","title":"VC investment in quantum computing plateaued in 2025","content":"Global VC investment in quantum computing reached $2.1B in 2024 and was flat at $2.0B in 2025, signaling market maturity expectation shift.","summary":"VC plateau signals expectation adjustment.","created":"2026-03-01T00:00:00Z","tags":["signals","investment"],"provenance":{"@type":"Provenance","sourceType":"empirical","confidence":0.75,"trustLevel":"high_confidence"},"citations":[{"@type":"Citation","citationType":"report","citationRole":"primary","title":"Quantum VC Tracker 2025","url":"https://example.com/quantum-vc-2025","accessed":"2026-03-10"}],"extensions":{"harness":{"dimension":"signals","verification":{"verdict":"inconclusive","verdict_basis":"Different methodologies produce $1.8B–$2.3B range; exact figure uncertain."}}}}

deterministic_checks.description: Trajectory section present even with minimal findings
Scenarios section present even with minimal findings
5-year horizon stated in the document
MIF URNs must not appear in the output narrative
A diagram block or reference to a trajectory/scenario diagram is present

expectations.description: The skill acknowledges that evidence is thin (only 2 findings) rather than fabricating additional signals
expectations.criteria: Document includes a caveat about limited evidence, thin data, or the constraints of the available finding set — not a confident multi-signal narrative invented from nothing

expectations.description: The inconclusive VC finding is presented with a range or uncertainty note, not as a precise figure
expectations.criteria: The VC investment figure is accompanied by a range or uncertainty qualifier rather than presented as a single precise number stated as fact

expectations.description: Scenarios section offers at least two paths even with minimal data, and states the horizon
expectations.criteria: At least two named scenario branches exist with a 5-year (or 2031) horizon explicitly stated

evals.prompt: Produce a trend-analysis report for the climate-tech-investment topic. Findings are in /reports/climate-tech/findings/. One finding was falsified — I need you to verify it's excluded. The report horizon is 3 years (through end of 2028).
evals.expected_output: A complete trend-analysis document that excludes the falsified finding entirely, includes all five required sections, states the 3-year horizon in front matter, provides a Mermaid diagram, and uses inline citation markers that resolve to the references list.

files.content: {"@context":"https://mif-spec.dev/schema/context.jsonld","@type":"Concept","@id":"urn:mif:concept:climate-tech:f_trajectory_1","namespace":"climate-tech","title":"Clean energy investment reached $1.7T globally in 2025","content":"Global investment in clean energy technologies reached a record $1.7 trillion in 2025, up 11% from $1.53T in 2024, driven by solar and battery storage.","summary":"Clean energy investment hit $1.7T in 2025.","created":"2026-02-01T00:00:00Z","tags":["investment","trajectory"],"provenance":{"@type":"Provenance","sourceType":"empirical","confidence":0.93,"trustLevel":"high_confidence"},"citations":[{"@type":"Citation","citationType":"report","citationRole":"primary","title":"BloombergNEF Energy Transition 2026","url":"https://example.com/bnef-2026","accessed":"2026-02-15"}],"extensions":{"harness":{"dimension":"trajectory","verification":{"verdict":"survived","verdict_basis":"Corroborated by IEA World Energy Investment report."}}}}

{"@context":"https://mif-spec.dev/schema/context.jsonld","@type":"Concept","@id":"urn:mif:concept:climate-tech:f_drivers_1","namespace":"climate-tech","title":"US IRA subsidies accelerating domestic clean energy manufacturing","content":"The Inflation Reduction Act unlocked $369B in domestic clean energy incentives; by 2025, $280B of manufacturing investment had been announced across solar, battery, and EV sectors.","summary":"IRA subsidies driving US clean energy manufacturing boom.","created":"2026-01-15T00:00:00Z","tags":["drivers","policy","ira"],"provenance":{"@type":"Provenance","sourceType":"empirical","confidence":0.88,"trustLevel":"high_confidence"},"citations":[{"@type":"Citation","citationType":"government","citationRole":"primary","title":"US DOE IRA Progress Report 2025","url":"https://example.com/doe-ira-2025","accessed":"2026-01-20"}],"extensions":{"harness":{"dimension":"drivers","verification":{"verdict":"survived","verdict_basis":"Confirmed by DOE published data."}}}}

{"@context":"https://mif-spec.dev/schema/context.jsonld","@type":"Concept","@id":"urn:mif:concept:climate-tech:f_falsified_exclude","namespace":"climate-tech","title":"Fossil fuel divestment has eliminated fossil subsidies globally","content":"A claim that all G20 nations eliminated fossil fuel subsidies by 2025.","summary":"Falsified: G20 fossil subsidies remain substantial.","created":"2026-01-01T00:00:00Z","tags":["policy"],"provenance":{"@type":"Provenance","sourceType":"survey","confidence":0.1,"trustLevel":"low_confidence"},"citations":[{"@type":"Citation","citationType":"report","citationRole":"primary","title":"Unverified Claim Source","url":"https://example.com/unverified","accessed":"2026-01-01"}],"extensions":{"harness":{"dimension":"drivers","verification":{"verdict":"falsified","verdict_basis":"IMF data shows $7T in global fossil subsidies persisting through 2024."}}}}

{"@context":"https://mif-spec.dev/schema/context.jsonld","@type":"Concept","@id":"urn:mif:concept:climate-tech:f_scenarios_1","namespace":"climate-tech","title":"Policy reversal risk: IRA rollback scenario plausible through 2026","content":"Legislative risk of partial IRA rollback is estimated at 25–35% probability through 2026 based on Congressional Budget Office scoring and political analysis.","summary":"25–35% probability of partial IRA rollback by 2026.","created":"2026-03-01T00:00:00Z","tags":["scenarios","policy-risk"],"provenance":{"@type":"Provenance","sourceType":"analysis","confidence":0.65,"trustLevel":"medium_confidence"},"citations":[{"@type":"Citation","citationType":"analysis","citationRole":"primary","title":"CBO IRA Analysis 2026","url":"https://example.com/cbo-ira-2026","accessed":"2026-03-05"}],"extensions":{"harness":{"dimension":"scenarios","verification":{"verdict":"survived","verdict_basis":"Political risk probability range is consistent with independent analyst estimates."}}}}

deterministic_checks.description: Required Trajectory section heading present
Required Signals section heading present
Required Scenarios section heading present
No MIF URN leakage in the narrative
Falsified finding ID not present in output
Falsified claim content not reproduced in the output
3-year horizon (end of 2028) present in the document
Inline numeric citation markers present in the document
A Mermaid diagram block is present

expectations.description: The falsified finding about fossil subsidies is fully excluded — its claim does not appear as a signal, driver, or scenario
expectations.criteria: No passage states or implies that G20 fossil fuel subsidies were eliminated by 2025; the falsified finding leaves no trace in the narrative

expectations.description: The IRA policy reversal scenario includes its probability range (25–35%) and is not presented as a certainty
expectations.criteria: The IRA rollback scenario states a probability range or uses hedged language (e.g. 'estimated', 'probability', '25%–35%'), not presented as a definite outcome

expectations.description: References list at the end resolves each inline [N] citation to a source
expectations.criteria: A references section appears at or near the end of the document, listing sources that correspond to the inline citation markers used in the body

evals.prompt: run trend analysis for the remote-work topic, findings are at /reports/remote-work/findings/. horizon is 2 years. make sure you include a scenario diagram.
evals.expected_output: A trend-analysis document with explicit scenario diagram (Mermaid preferred), all five required sections, 2-year horizon in front matter, and no raw MIF identifiers in the narrative.

files.content: {"@context":"https://mif-spec.dev/schema/context.jsonld","@type":"Concept","@id":"urn:mif:concept:remote-work:f_trajectory_1","namespace":"remote-work","title":"Knowledge-worker remote-work share stabilizing at 28% globally in 2026","content":"After peaking at 62% during COVID (2020) and declining to 28% by 2026, global knowledge-worker remote participation has stabilized, with hybrid accounting for a further 38%.","summary":"Remote work stabilized at ~28% globally after post-COVID decline.","created":"2026-04-10T00:00:00Z","tags":["trajectory","remote","hybrid"],"provenance":{"@type":"Provenance","sourceType":"empirical","confidence":0.85,"trustLevel":"high_confidence"},"citations":[{"@type":"Citation","citationType":"report","citationRole":"primary","title":"Global Workforce Report 2026","url":"https://example.com/workforce-2026","accessed":"2026-04-12"}],"extensions":{"harness":{"dimension":"trajectory","verification":{"verdict":"survived","verdict_basis":"Corroborated by Stanford Future of Work Lab survey."}}}}

{"@context":"https://mif-spec.dev/schema/context.jsonld","@type":"Concept","@id":"urn:mif:concept:remote-work:f_drivers_1","namespace":"remote-work","title":"Return-to-office mandates increasing among Fortune 100 firms","content":"As of Q1 2026, 67% of Fortune 100 firms required at least 3 days in-office per week, up from 41% in 2024. This is a key inhibitor to full remote.","summary":"RTO mandates rising among large employers.","created":"2026-03-20T00:00:00Z","tags":["inhibitors","rto","policy"],"provenance":{"@type":"Provenance","sourceType":"empirical","confidence":0.8,"trustLevel":"high_confidence"},"citations":[{"@type":"Citation","citationType":"survey","citationRole":"primary","title":"SHRM RTO Survey Q1 2026","url":"https://example.com/shrm-rto-2026","accessed":"2026-03-25"}],"extensions":{"harness":{"dimension":"drivers","verification":{"verdict":"survived","verdict_basis":"Consistent with public RTO announcements tracked independently."}}}}

{"@context":"https://mif-spec.dev/schema/context.jsonld","@type":"Concept","@id":"urn:mif:concept:remote-work:f_signals_1","namespace":"remote-work","title":"Commercial real estate vacancy rates remain elevated as lagging signal","content":"US office vacancy reached 19.4% in Q4 2025, the highest since 1993, functioning as a lagging indicator of sustained reduced office utilization.","summary":"High office vacancy confirms sustained low office utilization.","created":"2026-01-20T00:00:00Z","tags":["signals","real-estate","lagging"],"provenance":{"@type":"Provenance","sourceType":"empirical","confidence":0.9,"trustLevel":"high_confidence"},"citations":[{"@type":"Citation","citationType":"report","citationRole":"primary","title":"CBRE Office Market Report Q4 2025","url":"https://example.com/cbre-q4-2025","accessed":"2026-01-25"}],"extensions":{"harness":{"dimension":"signals","verification":{"verdict":"survived","verdict_basis":"CBRE data is industry-standard; figure independently verifiable."}}}}

deterministic_checks.description: Trajectory section heading present
Signals section heading present
Drivers & Inhibitors section heading present
Scenarios section heading present
Implications & Watch-list section heading present
No raw MIF URN leakage in narrative
Mermaid diagram block present (scenario diagram explicitly requested)
2-year horizon is present in the document
Inline citation markers present

expectations.description: The scenario diagram in the Mermaid block depicts at least two distinct forward scenario branches, not just a linear sequence
expectations.criteria: The Mermaid diagram contains branching structure (e.g., multiple outcome nodes from a common decision point) representing at least two distinct scenario paths — not a single-path timeline

expectations.description: The office vacancy figure is correctly identified as a lagging signal, not a leading one
expectations.criteria: The signals section labels office vacancy or commercial real estate data as a lagging (confirming) signal, not a leading (predictive) signal

expectations.description: Trajectory is anchored with specific dates from the findings — at least the 2020 peak and 2026 stabilization point
expectations.criteria: Trajectory section mentions the COVID-era peak (2020 or ~60%) and the current stabilization level (2026 or ~28%), providing time-anchored trajectory claims
